summary: Descriptive overview of actualcausation in substantive law: but-for test, substantial factor, multiple sufficient causes.
key_points: but-for test is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for actualcausation are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine actualcausation in litigation.
The doctrine of actualcausation has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding actualcausation requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
